t_ch: 世界聚焦于我们！
t_en: The world's watchful gaze fixes upon us!

t_ch: 我们平凡的灵魂…
t_en: Ordinary souls, yet destined to shine...

t_ch: 紧紧跟随不许多想…
t_en: Just follow the master tight, no excess thoughts allowed...

t_ch: 超越自我，引爆能量！
t_en: Transcendence attained, energies unbound!

t_ch: 勇于亮剑，谱写神章！
t_en: Brave and relentless, a divine chapter profound!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}的拍照技术不错嘛，是不是和三月小姐待久了也耳濡目染了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s camera skills aren't too bad. You've been hanging out with Miss March, haven't you, and picked up things from her?

t_ch: 确实值得称赞。
t_en: It really is commendable.

t_ch: 嗯…为你单独开一个编外摄影师的岗位，也不是不行。
t_en: Hmm... We could always set up a separate role for you as a freelance photographer.

t_ch: 公司的专机应该就快到了。那么就此别过吧，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
t_en: Our private jet should be arriving soon. So let's say so long for now, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 容许我再次向无名客的各位——尤其是姬子女士——表达感谢。希望在不远的将来，战略投资部能继续与各位*深度*合作。
t_en: Please allow me once again to express my gratitude to all of you Nameless, especially Miss Himeko. I hope that the Strategic Investment Department will continue our deep commitment to cooperation into the future.

t_ch: 那么，有缘再见！
t_en: So then, 'til we meet again!

t_ch: 哼唧！哼唧！
t_en: Oink! Oink!

t_ch: （深度合作…真不知道下次又会遇到战略投资部的哪位鬼才。）
t_en: (Deep cooperation... I wonder which eccentric from the Strategic Investment Department I'll meet next time?)

t_ch: （怎么感觉有点累了…原来大堂经理这份工作这么消耗精力吗？）
t_en: (Why am I feeling a little tired? ...Who knew being a lobby manager could be so tiring?)

t_ch: （挺住，{NICKNAME}…再坚持几个系统时就能下班了。）
t_en: (Hang in there, {NICKNAME}... Just a few more system hours, and you can clock out.)

t_ch: 哎呀呀…技术挺不错的嘛！
t_en: Wow... you're good at this!

t_ch: 果然，经你之手的造物都被「纯美」所眷顾…我钦佩你，我的挚友。
t_en: Indeed, all your creations are blessed by the Beauty... You have my admiration, my friend.

t_ch: …你拍马屁的功底还真浑然天成，银枝兄弟。
t_en: ...You're such a natural when it comes to flattery, Argenti.

t_ch: 这次我在匹诺康尼的事都已经办妥，接下来就该上路寻找下一个目标了。伙计们，有缘再聚！
t_en: I'm done with my affairs on Penacony, and I'm ready to move on to the next destination. Till we meet again, folks!

t_ch: 波提欧兄弟、青雀小姐、以及我的挚友{NICKNAME}…愿伊德莉拉的光芒照耀你们未来的路途。希世难得号已空置太久，我也该继续踏上追寻「纯美」的征程了。
t_en: Boothill, Miss Qingque, and {NICKNAME}... May your future be illuminated by Idrila's glow. The One and Only has been idle for too long. It's time for me to continue my quest to pursue the Beauty.

t_ch: 唔，这就到告别的时间了啊……
t_en: Oh? Can't believe it's already time for us to part ways...

t_ch: 你不舍得？
t_en: Are you feeling sad?

t_ch: 你还有什么感言？
t_en: Anything else you want to say?

t_ch: 呃，那倒不是…只是觉得有点怪，好像缺了点什么？
t_en: Um, not really... This just feels a bit odd, like something's missing.

t_ch: 你想啊，一般来说，一段故事到了最后不都该有个…叫什么来着，哦对，一般不都该有个「升华主题」的环节吗？
t_en: Look, when a story reaches the end, there's usually a part... What is it called? Ah, a meaningful conclusion that wraps everything up.

t_ch: 但这次的过程好像也太…顺利了点？你看啊，那个罗伊罗伊被你们打败以后就直接灰溜溜地跑了，也没有哭着喊着求饶、或者讲一大堆他自己的难处之类的……
t_en: But things have gone too... smoothly this time. Think about it — that Royroy just slunk away after being defeated by you. He neither cried for mercy nor prattled on about his grievances...

t_ch: 恕我直言，青雀女士，即便那所谓矲主有意倾吐他的苦衷，我和波提欧兄弟也会就地将他打断。
t_en: With all due respect, Miss Qingque, even if that so-called master had intended to confide his troubles, Boothill and I would have cut him off right there and then.

t_ch: 哈哈！这回咱俩总算想到一块去了，银枝兄弟！
t_en: Haha! We're finally on the same page this time, Argenti!

t_ch: 因为他建立这个组织的目的便是谋财害人，任何背景或苦衷都不能为其辩解——于我等骑士和游侠的眼中，那便是必被铲除之恶。
t_en: He made this organization to scam money out of people and harm everyone. So, nothing about his background or any excuses can justify his actions. As a knight and a ranger, we see it as an evil that must be eliminated at all costs.

t_ch: （出发吧…去梦境酒店把青雀捞出来！）
t_en: (Let's go... Time to go rescue Qingque from the hotel!)

t_ch: 出发！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: 再等等…
t_en: Wait...

t_ch: 为、为了您的安全着想，请您放弃闯入的想法！
t_en: F—For the sake of your safety, please drop the idea of breaking in!

t_ch: （硬闯看来不是好主意…还是去启动小小哈努行动的电视机吧。）
t_en: (Barging in might not be a good idea... I better go activate that Hanu's Adventure television set instead.)

t_ch: 班上得如何了？
t_en: How's work going for you?

t_ch: 呃…不瞒您说，最近入住白日梦酒店的客人数量明显变少了，我头一次感觉到工作如此清闲。
t_en: Er... just between us, the number of guests at The Reverie has drastically dropped recently. It's my first time experiencing such a lull at work.

t_ch: 对我来说这可是件大好事…您知道为什么吗？因为这样一来，我就有时间多刷刷帝垣琼玉的视频，增长牌技了。
t_en: It's actually a blessing in disguise for me... Why, you ask? Because now I'll have more time to watch Celestial Jade videos and improve my skills.

t_ch: 您如果也对帝垣琼玉感兴趣的话，下次有空我给您推荐几个博主——很有意思的，我向您保证！
t_en: If you're also into Celestial Jade, then I'll recommend some content creators for you when I get around to it — I guarantee you'll find them super intriguing!

t_ch: 你可算来了，等你好久了！差点以为你赶不上了……
t_en: You're finally here. I've been waiting for ages! I started to think you might not make it...

t_ch: 抱歉，我来晚了，今天我买单…
t_en: Sorry, I'm late. I'll pay the bill...

t_ch: 又不是吃饭来晚了，今天没有买单的环节啦。
t_en: We're not going out for dinner. There are no bills to pay today!

t_ch: 三月，你让我感到很大的压力…
t_en: March, I feel like you're putting a lot of pressure on me...

t_ch: 是吗？抱歉。我带了苏打豆汁儿，你要不要先喝一罐解压？
t_en: Really? Sorry. I brought some mung bean soda with me. Why not have a can to take the edge off?

t_ch: 主人公从来都是最后登场的嘛！
t_en: It's only right that the hero is last to board!

t_ch: 哪有这么好用的迟到理由啊！
t_en: Well, that's a convenient excuse for bad time-keeping!

t_ch: 不必着急，{NICKNAME}老师来得正是时候。
t_en: There is no rush. You're here just in time, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 列车组成员也算是往返罗浮多次了，倒也并不需要什么送别，我们几个随意一些也无妨。三月说你差点赶不上的另有其他的事情——
t_en: The crew have run to and from the Luofu many times, so there's no need to go to all the effort of a formal send-off, surely? March said you were late because you had something else —

t_ch: 你知道吗？曜青仙舟、朱明仙舟的使者就要回去了！
t_en: Didn't you know? The Xianzhou Yaoqing and the Xianzhou Zhuming messengers are heading back home!

t_ch: 第一次听说！
t_en: I hadn't heard that!

t_ch: 抱歉，都怪我没有在短信里仔细说明。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't explain properly in my message.

t_ch: 没想到是今天。
t_en: I had no idea that was today.

t_ch: …这也是我希望列车的各位老师能来的原因。
t_en: ...That was why I hoped that the crew would be here.

t_ch: 他们一直留在罗浮不好吗？
t_en: Can't they just stay on the Luofu forever?

t_ch: 粉毛狐狸、鸦羽怪人、云璃师父就这样留下…倒还好。
t_en: The pink-haired fox, the crow-feather weirdo, and Master Yunli staying... That's still not bad, I suppose.

t_ch: 那两位将军肯定不能一直留在罗浮的嘛！
t_en: But the two generals cannot possibly stay on the Luofu forever!

t_ch: 于是我提议，列车组的送别就留在最后吧！咱们先和彦卿一起送别远道而来的客人！
t_en: That's why I suggested that we save the Crew's farewell until the very end! Let's go and see off our guests from afar on their way with Yanqing.

t_ch: 简直就是尽地主之谊！
t_en: You make it sound like we're the hosts here!

t_ch: 这里是丹恒的老家，咱们当然也算是地主啦。
t_en: This is Dan Heng's home. Of course we count as hosts as well!

t_ch: 你看，我在这里拜师学剑，你在这里也结识了不少人…罗浮，四舍五入也算是咱们的老家了。
t_en: I studied swordsmanship here. Plus, think about how many friends you've made here... The Luofu is basically our home too.

t_ch: 我也想和他们一一告别。
t_en: I want to... say goodbye, too.

t_ch: 对吧对吧！咱就猜你也是这样想的，没等你来就决定了！
t_en: Right! We knew you'd feel this way, so we went ahead and decided for you!

t_ch: 所以，三月其实是怕你赶不上告别。
t_en: That's why March was scared. She thought you'd miss getting to say goodbye.

t_ch: 我猜你肯定会后悔的！
t_en: I knew you'd regret it!

t_ch: 这下明白了！
t_en: I get it now!

t_ch: 谢谢你，三月！
t_en: Thank you, March!

t_ch: 不用谢！
t_en: No worries!

t_ch: 先前天击将军、烛渊将军已在神策府与景元将军作别。二位将军希望轻车简从，不需要云骑军列阵过于引人注目。本来彦卿觉得独自前来临舟饯别，未免有些失礼……
t_en: The Merlin's Claw and the Flaming Heart have already said their farewells to General Jing Yuan at the Seat of Divine Foresight. They wanted to travel light without the Cloud Knights' escort so as not to attract too much attention. I originally thought it may be a little rude to come to bid them farewell on my own, but...

t_ch: 如今有诸位列车客人同列，我也感觉安心不少。既然老师们有如此心意，咱们一起在这里稍作等候。
t_en: I feel much more at ease together with the Express guests. As you all feel the same way, let's wait here together for a while.

t_ch: 姬子小姐和瓦尔特先生也已经从「金伦加深域」返航，今日也将抵达罗浮仙舟。
t_en: Miss Himeko and Mr. Yang have already begun their return from the Calenga Abyss as well, and they'll be arriving at the Luofu today.

t_ch: 要是时间凑巧，我们应该是能在星槎海港口上见面的。
t_en: If the timing is right, we should be able to meet them at the Starskiff Haven port.

t_ch: 咱们列队欢迎他们！
t_en: Let's line up to welcome them!

t_ch: 倒也不必如此隆重…哎……
t_en: I don't think we need all this fuss... Ah...

t_ch: 这可真是好时机啊！
t_en: This is a great opportunity!

t_ch: 我也不知道那算不算好时机……
t_en: I don't know if I'd call it that...

t_ch: 离他们来还有一段时间，现在先专心送别仙舟的客人吧。
t_en: We still have some time before they arrive. Let's focus on sending off our Xianzhou guests for now.

t_ch: 时间匆忙，我只买了一些老罗浮人都爱喝的饮料作为伴手礼。
t_en: I didn't have much time, so the only souvenir I managed to buy was the Luofu people's favorite drink.

t_ch: 你想他们远道而来，回到其他仙舟肯定也要花不少时间，这个时候口渴了，就可以喝上地道的罗浮口味……
t_en: If they're visitors from afar, then I imagine returning to their Xianzhou will take a long time. If they get thirsty, they can drink this and get a taste of authentic Luofu flavor...

t_ch: 不会是「苏打豆汁儿」吧？
t_en: It's not mung bean soda, is it?

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 不会是「仙人快乐茶」吧？
t_en: It's not Immortal's Delight, is it?

t_ch: 你懂不懂老罗浮人喜欢喝什么呀？肯定是「苏打豆汁儿」啊！
t_en: Do you not know the people of the Luofu by now? Of course it's mung bean soda!

t_ch: 我有点担心路上喝了会吐出来…
t_en: I'm worried they'll be sick if they drink that on the journey...

t_ch: 有这么难喝吗？这都是心意！
t_en: Is it really that disgusting? This is a gift from the kindness of my heart!

t_ch: 老罗浮的口味，居家旅行必备。
t_en: The taste of the Luofu, a must-have anywhere.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，我看它的广告也是这么说的。
t_en: Hehehe, I saw that advert, too!

t_ch: 咱不能让客人们空手回去嘛！
t_en: We can't send our guests off empty-handed!

t_ch: 按接驳客船的时间来看，曜青的客人已经快到了。
t_en: Judging by the standard vessel transfer time, the Yaoqing guests should be arriving at any moment.

t_ch: 明白了，我就在这里等候。
t_en: Okay, I'll wait here.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还有些事情。
t_en: Wait a moment. There's something I need to do.

t_ch: 过了不久，曜青仙舟的客人来到了港口……
t_en: After a few moments, the Yaoqing guests arrived at the port...

t_ch: 这不是我们罗浮仙舟的守擂冠军彦卿小弟吗？哦，星穹列车的朋友也在。
t_en: If it isn't Yanqing, the Xianzhou Luofu reigning champion! Oh, and our Astral Express friends are here, too!

t_ch: 拜别飞霄将军。
t_en: Farewell, General Feixiao.

t_ch: 拜别飞霄将军。
t_en: Farewell, General Feixiao.

t_ch: 拜别飞霄将军。
t_en: Farewell, General Feixiao.

t_ch: 很可惜，没能跟将军交手。
t_en: It is a shame that we never got that duel.

t_ch: 元帅三令五申，不准我技痒登台。不过看到有实力的盟友却不能切磋一番，我也觉得很可惜。
t_en: The Marshal warned me repeatedly that I was not permitted to take to the stage and show off my skills. Although, I also think it's a shame not being able to spar with my talented allies.

t_ch: 这样吧，过些日子，我托人将拜战帖送到星穹列车上。
t_en: How about I have someone send an invitation to battle to the Astral Express when I get back?

t_ch: 哎，我刚才是开玩笑的。
t_en: Ah, I was just joking...

t_ch: ……我可没有。
t_en: ...I wasn't.

t_ch: 好，我会尽全力讨教的。
t_en: Alright, I'll do my best to learn from you.

t_ch: …不过最近联盟风波未定，还有很多事要做好准备，交手之日可能会隔上很久。
t_en: ...Although, the recent alliance turmoil is yet to settle and there are lots of preparations to take care of. I imagine we will have to wait some time for our battle.

t_ch: 希望是一万年。
t_en: Make it ten thousand years.

t_ch: 在时间面前，谁都是输家。
t_en: There are no winners in the face of time.

t_ch: 别让我等太久。
t_en: Don't keep me waiting too long.

t_ch: 好啊，希望我们都能拼尽全力。
t_en: Alright! I hope we will all do our best.

t_ch: 离开罗浮之前还能跟诸位见上一面，实在是飞霄的荣幸。
t_en: I am truly honored that I was able to meet with you all one more time before I left the Luofu.

t_ch: 对了！我给大家准备了罗浮仙舟最地道的饮料——苏打豆汁儿，请大家不要客气，在路上口渴的时候喝！
t_en: Oh yeah! I brought authentic local drinks from the Xianzhou Luofu for you all — mung bean soda! Please don't hesitate to take one, and you can enjoy it if you get thirsty on your journeys!

t_ch: 三月七熟络地将她准备好的苏打豆汁儿拿出，地道的罗浮饮料已经准备很久了！
t_en: March 7th takes out the mung bean soda she prepared with practiced ease. She'd prepared this authentic Luofu drink ages ago!

t_ch: 不久之后，曜青的客人就登上星槎离开了港口。
t_en: And just like that, the Yaoqing guests boarded their starskiff and left the port.

t_ch: 我不擅长在这种场合下发表感受，就交给你和三月了。
t_en: I am not good at expressing my feelings at times like this. I'll leave the talking to you, March.

t_ch: 别担心，罗浮特产苏打豆汁儿还剩的很多！
t_en: Don't worry. I still have lots of Luofu's specialty mung bean soda left!

t_ch: 按接驳客船的时间来看，朱明的客人已经快到了。
t_en: Judging by the standard vessel transfer time, the Zhuming guests should be arriving at any moment.

t_ch: 明白了，我就在这里等候。
t_en: Okay, I'll wait here.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还有些事情。
t_en: Wait a moment. There's something I need to do.

t_ch: 过了不久，朱明仙舟的客人来到了港口……
t_en: After a few moments, the Zhuming guests arrived at the port...

t_ch: 是彦卿小兄弟，还有星穹列车的客人们啊，让各位久等了。
t_en: It's Yanqing and the Astral Express crew! Thank you all for waiting.

t_ch: 拜别怀炎将军。
t_en: I bid you farewell, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 拜别怀炎将军。
t_en: I bid you farewell, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 拜别怀炎将军。
t_en: I bid you farewell, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 很可惜，没能跟将军交手。
t_en: It is a shame that we never got that duel.

t_ch: 哦？想见识我的身手？
t_en: Oh? You wish to see my skills?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，难得能见到这么不知死活的年轻人。一句话把老夫唤回了那个热血还能沸腾的岁月里，真是教人畅怀啊。
t_en: Hahaha! It is rare to find a youngster that has such little fear of death. Just being asked to battle takes me back to those years when my blood burned like fire. How refreshing.

t_ch: 我刚才是开玩笑的。
t_en: I was just joking.

t_ch: 巧了，老夫也是说笑的。
t_en: Oh good, me too.

t_ch: 我对将军的实力十分好奇。
t_en: I am very curious about your true strength, General.

t_ch: 唉，可不要因为飞霄就对我怀有什么误会啊。
t_en: Ah, please don't allow Feixiao to mislead you about me.

t_ch: 老朽只是醉心匠艺，对争胜斗狠之事的兴趣早已乏了。
t_en: I am but an old man that has grown to love craftsmanship. My interest in competitions and fighting beasts faded long ago.

t_ch: 还是让你们年轻人之间多聊几句吧！云璃，来之前你不是准备了很多话要说吗？
t_en: But I want to hear more from the youngsters! Yunli, did you not have lots prepared that you wanted to say before you left?

t_ch: 咳咳，三月是我的徒弟，临走之前还是得嘱咐几句嘛。
t_en: Ahem, as a master, I should pass on some words of advice to March before I leave.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，云璃师父！
t_en: Hehe, Master Yunli!

t_ch: 说起来，三月你是不是没有参加演武仪典？
t_en: Come to think of it, you never took part in the Wardance in the end, did you?

t_ch: 啊，这个，确实…没有参加。
t_en: Ah, well, no... I didn't.

t_ch: 你和三月小姐不都在丹鼎司里养伤吗？
t_en: Weren't you and March both being treated in the Alchemy Commission at the time?

t_ch: 我能不知道吗？我的意思是说，既然没有完成最初的教学目标，三月就还是个没出师的徒弟。哪怕我回到朱明仙舟，三月你可不能疏忽了练习！
t_en: You think I don't know that? What I mean is, as she has not yet completed the initial target for her training, March has still not completed her tuition. I don't want you slacking on your training while I'm back on the Xianzhou Zhuming!

t_ch: 别担心，我会帮忙监督的。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll keep an eye on her.

t_ch: 很好，不过你容易惯着她！
t_en: Good, no spoiling her like you normally do!

t_ch: 就算出师了，她也得好好练习。
t_en: Even if she had, she would still need to train.

t_ch: 有道理，剑术锻炼是不可以怠慢的！
t_en: Quite right! Swordsmanship practice cannot be neglected!

t_ch: 话说云璃师父，你一开始是奔着怀炎将军的奖品来的吧，没能参加演武仪典不遗憾吗？
t_en: Come to think of it, Master Yunli, weren't you originally after the reward that General Huaiyan was offering? Aren't you sad you weren't able to take part in the Wardance?

t_ch: 也没那么遗憾啦！我听说彦卿守擂得胜，宝剑也算是握在合适的人手中。
t_en: I'm not that sad! I heard Yanqing took victory as the ringmaster. So the sword still rests in worthy hands.

t_ch: 何况，爷爷带着奖品来，我赢下擂台又把奖品带了回去，实在缺了礼数。这一回我就不抢彦卿小弟的风头了，免得他下不了台。
t_en: Plus, my grandfather has brought the prize all the way here. So does it not seem a little unceremonious for me to just win it and take it straight back? I will not steal Yanqing's thunder this time, as I don't want to embarrass him.

t_ch: 哼，谢谢云璃姑娘高抬贵手，保全我罗浮剑士的面子。
t_en: Hmph, such gracious generosity, Yunli. You really helped me save face as the Luofu swordmaster.

t_ch: 我天，爱剑如命的彦卿居然…
t_en: Woah, Yanqing loves that sword more than his life, and yet...

t_ch: 送礼还真是门学问呐…
t_en: Gift-giving is quite an art...

t_ch: 天呐！彦卿师父不会想把剑送给云璃师父吧？
t_en: Woah! Are you really thinking about giving your sword to Yunli, Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 真的吗？这把剑可是你在演武仪典竞锋得胜的证明啊……一想到之前拿走了你的剑，我都有些过意不去了。
t_en: Really? This sword is your proof of victory in the Wardance... Now I feel bad about taking your sword before.

t_ch: 好，好小子！老夫没看走眼啊。
t_en: What generosity! I knew I was right about you.

t_ch: 云璃姑娘说笑了，这是彦卿守擂赢来的。想到你们要分别了，很长时间见不到了，我心说得拿过来给你摸摸，聊慰遗憾。
t_en: You're such the humorist, Yunli. This was the sword I won as the ringmaster. I was just thinking that as you were leaving, you would not see it for a while. I brought it so you could touch it one last time and ease your regrets.

t_ch: 我天，彦卿真的爱剑如命！
t_en: Wow, Yanqing really does love that sword more than his life!

t_ch: 说话还真是门学问呐…
t_en: Communication is quite an art...

t_ch: 我给云璃师父翻译翻译：「欸嘿，这是我的剑，不给」。
t_en: Let me translate for you, Master Yunli: "Nuh-uh, this is my sword, and you can't have it."

t_ch: 啧…这小子，有我当年的风范。
t_en: Tsk... This boy, he's just like me at this age.

t_ch: 彦…卿…小…弟，你这算是伤口上撒盐吧？！啊？！
t_en: You... Yanqing! You just wanted to rub salt in my wounds! Huh?

t_ch: 听好了，等你休养好了，记得带着剑来朱明找我。到时候我会给你打一柄好剑作为赔礼…不过嘛，规矩你懂的，你得与我以剑相会，堂堂正正地赢下它。怎么样？不许不来！
t_en: You listen to me. Once you've finished resting, bring that sword to the Zhuming and find me. I will forge a good sword as apology... However, you know the rules. You must meet me with steel and win the sword fair and square. Got that? It's a promise!

t_ch: 经此一役，彦卿心头也有了远游增广见闻的打算。既然云璃姑娘邀请，那么我们一言为定。
t_en: Recent events have inspired me to travel afar and expand my knowledge of this life. As you so kindly invited me, Yunli, it's a promise.

t_ch: 就在朱明仙舟客人将行之际，灵砂也正巧来到港口。
t_en: Lingsha happens to arrive at the harbor just as the Zhuming guests are about to leave.

t_ch: 不久之后，朱明的客人就登上星槎离开了港口，灵砂大人也回到了丹鼎司。
t_en: And just like that, the Zhuming guests boarded their starskiff and left the port, and Lingsha returned to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 一艘星槎静静地停靠过来，从舱门里走出来的正是你们熟悉的符玄太卜。
t_en: A starskiff silently pulls to a stop as the familiar figure of Master Diviner Fu Xuan emerges from the hatch.

t_ch: 本座专注于卜算种种事端因果，却没有算到回到仙舟，竟然有如此多的人夹道迎接。果然，这罗浮仙舟还是不能少了本座呀。
t_en: I have been devoted to divining the cause and effect outcomes of so many things, yet I never divined that there would be so many people here to meet me on my return to the Xianzhou. I see the Xianzhou Luofu really just can't go on without me.

t_ch: 符太卜，我们在这等你很久了。
t_en: Diviner Fu, we have been waiting a long time.

t_ch: 哼，有劳各位迎接，本座回来了。
t_en: Hmm, thank you for the warm welcome. I have returned.

t_ch: 符太卜，你怎么回来了？
t_en: Diviner Fu, what brings you back here?

t_ch: 啊？所以，你们在这里站着，跟本座，嗯…一点关系都没有吗？
t_en: Huh? So, you all standing here has... absolutely nothing to do with me?

t_ch: 这个粉毛矮个子是谁呀？
t_en: Who's this sassy, lost, short pink-haired girl?

t_ch: 哈？本座可不能当作没听见。
t_en: Huh? I cannot pretend I didn't hear that.

t_ch: …还是让我来解释一下吧。
t_en: ...Let me explain.

t_ch: 彦卿解释了仙舟贵客的送行经过，以及完全没想到符玄大人此时回来的「事实」。
t_en: Yanqing explained about seeing off the Xianzhou guests and the "reality" that they had no idea that Fu Xuan was returning.

t_ch: 事情的大致经过，本座已经了解了。虽然这是巧合，但是你们出现在这里也是不争的事实。不如我们各退一步…「各退一步」这词有点不合适，就当你们在迎接本座凯旋吧。
t_en: I understand what has happened. Although this was merely a coincidence, it is still a fact that you are all here now. How about you meet me halfway... "Meet me halfway" is a strange expression for this, but why don't we pretend you were awaiting my triumphant return?

t_ch: 话说彦卿就算了，你们在罗浮上待了这么久，就没意识到本座不在罗浮吗？
t_en: Actually, Yanqing, forget it. You have all been on the Luofu for all this time, and you didn't even notice that I wasn't here?

t_ch: 我注意到了，太卜司空落落的。
t_en: I noticed, the Divination Commission has been so empty and lonely.

t_ch: 很好，看来你是一个真正关心罗浮的人。
t_en: Very good. I see you truly care about the Luofu.

t_ch: 要是有符玄大人哪有这么多事！
t_en: If you were here, we would never have had so many issues!

t_ch: 正是，要是本座在这里，哪里会出这么多岔子！
t_en: Precisely. There's no way so many things would go wrong with me around!

t_ch: 完全没有注意到，以为你还在…
t_en: I didn't notice at all. I thought you were still here...

t_ch: 你听见了吗？刚才有什么东西碎裂开的声音…那是本座为罗浮仙舟操劳的心。
t_en: Did you hear that? It's a sound of something smashing to pieces... That was my heart thinking about working on the Xianzhou Luofu again.

t_ch: 要不是我在玉阙仙舟应对各种质询，景元他…算了，这件事不提也罢。
t_en: If I didn't have so many inquiries to deal with on the Xianzhou Yuque, then Jing Yuan would... Forget it, it's not important.

t_ch: 符玄大人累了吧，我这里还有苏打豆汁儿，给您接风洗尘！
t_en: You must be tired, Master Diviner Fu. I have some mung bean soda. Please take one cup as a welcome back gift!

t_ch: 谢谢，心意我领了。不过我对饮品还是有些挑剔，正常罗浮人应该是没人喝苏打豆汁儿的，待会本座请你品一下星芋啵啵，你自会明白。
t_en: Thank you. That is very kind. However, I am quite picky when it comes to drinks. Almost no regular Luofu citizen actually drinks mung bean soda. I'll take you to try some startaro bubble tea and you'll understand.

t_ch: 诶？
t_en: ...Hmm?

t_ch: 想打听下太卜大人的玉阙见闻。
t_en: What did you learn about the Yuque?

t_ch: 本座主要是辅助爻光将军，提供建木灾变的始末信息，以及弄清楚重犯镜流所言真假…说实话，基本没有什么休息时间，也谈不上见闻了。
t_en: I was mainly supporting General Yao Guang with providing details about the Ambrosial Arbor disaster and discerning the truth from the lies in Jingliu's confession... Honestly, I had no time to rest or learn anything new.

t_ch: 至于成果嘛，虽然你们贵为仙舟盟友，但此时此刻并非是揭露的最佳时机，本座还需要进一步与景元商讨…你们只需静候佳音就好。
t_en: As for the results, although you are all precious Xianzhou allies, this is not the best time to reveal all. I still need to discuss things with Jing Yuan so... You just await my good news.

t_ch: 呼雷越狱之事，太卜大人已知？
t_en: Did you hear about Hoolay's escape from prison, Master Diviner?

t_ch: 这件事沸沸扬扬的，本座自然是知道的。不过就像是仙舟民众一样，得知整件事都处在神策府、十王司的控制之中，结果上算得是振奋人心。
t_en: News about his escape has spread like wildfire. Of course, I am aware. Although, much like the citizens of the Xianzhou, I had nothing but joy when I learned that the entire matter was under the control of the Seat of Divine Foresight and the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 返程的路途中，本座大致卜算了一下事由经过，还得说幸好有你们，星穹列车的客人，罗浮仙舟又渡过了一劫。
t_en: I divined a rough idea of how things unfolded on my return journey and I must say: We are so fortunate to have you. Our guests from the Astral Express have saved the Xianzhou Luofu from disaster once more.

t_ch: 太卜大人要回来早告诉我们嘛！
t_en: You should have told us you were coming back!

t_ch: 本座也不是一个喜欢兴师动众的人，不过是工作出差回来，用不着这么多人夹道迎接。
t_en: I do not like fusses. This was simply a work trip, and there was no need for a welcome party.

t_ch: 不过，我倒是提前联系了青雀，让她来星槎海接我…哼，我看了看，她也不在这里，该回太卜司拿青雀是问了。
t_en: Although, I did contact Qingque in advance and tell her to come and meet me at Starskiff Haven... Hmph, I see she's nowhere to be seen. Looks like it's time to head back to the Divination Commission and see exactly what excuse she has this time.

t_ch: 青雀，快逃。
t_en: Qingque, run!

t_ch: 青雀，要怪就怪自己吧。
t_en: You brought this on yourself, Qingque.

t_ch: 没关系，正好彦卿打算向列车组送别，正好也是太卜大人的接风洗尘。咱们也一起拍张合照，这才是算完整。
t_en: It's okay! Yanqing was planning to say their farewells to the Crew, so he could be your welcoming party, too! We have to take a group picture together first though!

t_ch: 嗯，三月小姐的提议不错，就这样办吧。
t_en: Not a bad suggestion at all, Miss March. Let's do that.

t_ch: 列车组往返罗浮多次，即便我们是作为神策府邀请的客人，送别之事倒也不必太过郑重。今日小别，以后仙舟联盟若有事相商，请随时联系我们的列车长。
t_en: The Crew visits the Luofu all the time. Even if we are guests of the Seat of Divine Foresight, there's no need to go to all the effort of a formal send off. We'll say a goodbye for now, but if the Xianzhou alliance has anything further to discuss in the future, please don't hesitate to contact our conductor.

t_ch: 毕竟星穹列车与仙舟联盟已是盟友。
t_en: After all, the Astral Express is now in alliance with the Xianzhou Alliance.

t_ch: 不错，即便是跨越迢迢星海，远道而来的贵客，想要回到罗浮仙舟相见，也并非总是难事。我相信以后还有机会能与各位老师再次相见，讨教一番。
t_en: That's right. Though you may be our guests from far across the starry skies, it will not be difficult for us to meet again should you wish to return to the Xianzhou Luofu. I am certain that we will have a chance to meet again, and I can learn from you all once more.

t_ch: …说实话，彦卿也有搭乘列车四处巡游磨砺自己的打算。不知老师们意下如何？
t_en: ...Honestly, I also intend to board the Express and set off on my own adventure of self-improvement. I wonder what you would all say to that?

t_ch: 我们肯定是欢迎的呀！对了，姬子姐姐和杨叔，今天不是也要回来吗？我们还可以找他们商量商量……
t_en: You're more than welcome, of course! Oh yeah, weren't Himeko and Mr. Yang saying that they wanted to come along today, too? We could find them to discuss it further...

t_ch: 眼下彦卿还在养伤，也没做好出行准备，不急于此时。
t_en: I am still recovering from my wounds, so I am not yet ready to depart. This matter can wait for now.

t_ch: 嗯嗯。
t_en: Hmm...

t_ch: 看来是等不到列车家长组了？
t_en: Looks like our Express family aren't going to make it in time?

t_ch: 就不耽误各位时间了，解散！
t_en: Well, we don't want to keep you waiting around. Let's head out!

t_ch: 嗯，今日临舟饯别就到此为止吧，感谢罗浮仙舟的殷勤招待，丹恒代表列车组谢过了。
t_en: Indeed. Let's leave the farewell party here for today. On behalf of the Crew, I would like to thank the Xianzhou Luofu for your kind hospitality.

t_ch: 列车组的各位，一路顺风。
t_en: Safe travels, friends.

t_ch: 与此同时，港口的另一边。
t_en: At that moment, on the other side of the port.

t_ch: 即便演武仪典结束了，罗浮仙舟还是这么热闹啊…对了，小三月他们呢？
t_en: I can't believe the Xianzhou Luofu is still so busy even after the Wardance is over... Oh yeah, where are March and the others?

t_ch: 丹恒说他们已经在港口上等待着了，估计就在这附近吧？
t_en: Dan Heng said that they were waiting in the port. I imagine they must be nearby?

t_ch: 恭喜符玄大人在玉阙仙舟的出差工作中的出色表现和勇敢拼搏！特此，热烈庆祝罗浮仙舟太卜司唯一的光——太卜大人、暨罗浮仙舟神策将军候补，符玄大人凯旋回归！
t_en: Congratulations to Lady Fu for your outstanding performance and courageous efforts on the Xianzhou Yuque! I would hereby like to celebrate the triumphant return of Master Fu Xuan, known as the only light in the Xianzhou Luofu Divination Commission and the Divine Foresight's candidate!

t_ch: …小姑娘，你看清楚了，我们可不是符玄…大人？
t_en: ...Miss. Open your eyes. We are not... Master Fu Xuan?

t_ch: 诶？杨先生，旁边的，难道是姬子小姐？
t_en: Huh? Mr. Yang? And could that be.... Miss Himeko?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Indeed I am.

t_ch: 那太卜大人呢？
t_en: Then where is the Master Diviner?

t_ch: 我们也不知道，你们家太卜大人是这个时候到这里吗？
t_en: How would we know? When did your Master Diviner even get here?

t_ch: 难道说，我错过了？！
t_en: You don't mean... I missed her!?
Huuuuh—!?

t_ch: 曜青的客人就快到了…
t_en: The Yaoqing guests will be arriving soon.

t_ch: （要是做好了准备，就和彦卿一起等待接下来的客人吧……）
t_en: (If you are ready, await the next group of guests with Yanqing.)

t_ch: 朱明的客人就快到了…
t_en: The Zhuming guests will be arriving soon.

t_ch: 就等你就位了，快来！
t_en: We're all waiting for you, hurry up!

t_ch: 位置给你留好了！
t_en: We've left a spot for you!

t_ch: 快来，一起拍合照了！
t_en: C'mon, let's take a photo!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，站这里！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, stand here!

t_ch: 三月小姐，你真是太客气了，哈哈哈哈！
t_en: You are too kind, Miss March, hahaha!

t_ch: 我们在罗浮仙舟相识数日，已然是过命的交情。我明白各位临行送别时不舍的心意，不过有句话是这样说的——
t_en: Though we have known each other for only a number of days, we are already friends for life. I am just as reluctant to part as you all are, but there is a phrase I once heard...

t_ch: 如果没有离别，我们又如何珍惜相聚的时刻？如果没有离别，我们又如何感受重逢的喜悦。三月小姐，请你不要伤感，离别只会串联起我们相遇时种种疏忽的细节……
t_en: If we were to never part, how would we treasure our time together? If there were no farewell, how could we experience the joy of a reunion? Miss March, please do not be sad, as our parting will only connect the dots between the details we missed when we met.

t_ch: …椒丘大夫，其实我们在这里。
t_en: ...Master Jiaoqiu. We're actually over here.

t_ch: 刚才咱也在犹豫要不要提醒一下……
t_en: We were hesitating whether to tell you or not...

t_ch: 我猜三月没有考虑这么多感想…
t_en: I imagine March hadn't thought that much about all this...

t_ch: 我确实也没有那么多感受，不过椒丘大夫说得太好了！说的就是我。
t_en: I hadn't, and you're right. But you're absolutely right, Master Jiaoqiu. That's exactly how I feel.

t_ch: …顺带一提，我们其实是在这个方向。椒丘大夫，你在对着空气说话……
t_en: ...I should also probably mention, I'm actually over here. You've been talking to thin air, Master Jiaoqiu...

t_ch: 椒丘大夫，你说的真是太好了！
t_en: You expressed it perfectly, Master Jiaoqiu!

t_ch: 没错…顺带一提，我们其实是在这个方向。椒丘大夫，你在对着空气说话……
t_en: That's right... Though I should also probably mention, I'm actually over here. You've been talking to thin air, Master Jiaoqiu...

t_ch: 啊，是这样吗？啊哈哈哈，不好意思。
t_en: Ah, over here? Ahaha, I'm sorry.

t_ch: 我这不是还没习惯嘛，虽然眼睛不方便，但在下的耳朵可灵敏了！可以听声辨位。
t_en: I'm still not used to this yet. Though my eyes may not be of any use, my ears are extremely sensitive! I can pinpoint location through sound!

t_ch: …好像还是有点不太对，不过，就这样吧。{NICKNAME}快说点什么，冷场了！
t_en: ...I think you're still a little off but... Well, that's good enough. {NICKNAME}, say something quickly. There's an awkward silence!

t_ch: 演武仪典期间都在做些什么呢？
t_en: What were you doing during the Wardance?

t_ch: 在下自然是在养伤嘛。多亏了灵砂司鼎，衔药龙女的精湛医术，咱们也算是恢复得差不多了，接下来要做的，不过是「谨遵医嘱」四个字。
t_en: I was having my wounds treated, of course! Thanks to the Healer Lady, Cauldron Master Lingsha, we are returning to proper form. All we've been told to do now is "listen to doctor's orders."

t_ch: 我得空去看了彦卿的守擂赛，打得不错。更难得的是对对手的敬意。
t_en: I managed to get time to go and see Yanqing's ringmaster match. You did very well. Your respect for your adversary was particularly commendable, too.

t_ch: …多谢将军点评。
t_en: ...Thank you, general.

t_ch: 有去参加演武仪典的比赛吗？
t_en: Did you take part in the Wardance competition?

t_ch: 我的身份实在不适合参加这样的比赛，至于这边的两位嘛……
t_en: My identity makes it improper for me to take part in these kinds of competitions, but these two on the other hand...

t_ch: 别问我，我就是个医士，就算是眼睛方便的时候，我也不会凑这个热闹，你问问貊泽。
t_en: Don't ask me. I'm just a healer. You wouldn't catch me getting involved in that kind of thing even when my eyes were working. Ask Moze.

t_ch: 我喜欢暗中观察，伺机出手。不过很遗憾，演武仪典的赛制不会给我这么多时间。
t_en: I prefer to watch from the shadows and wait for my moment to strike. Unfortunately, the Wardance competition would not give me the time for this.

t_ch: 要不貊泽你再多聊两句？
t_en: Moze, how about you chat a little longer?
Uhh...

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 我还以为你们已经知道了，我是会把天聊死的。
t_en: I think you're all well aware that I'm a conversation killer.

t_ch: 唉，为了大家好，我们还是换一个话题吧。
t_en: *sigh* For everyone's well-being, we'd better change the topic.

t_ch: 这次罗浮仙舟之行，感受如何？
t_en: How do you feel about your journey to the Xianzhou Luofu?

t_ch: 罗浮之行的目的已经达成，虽然呼雷一事出乎预料…但好在一切解决的法子尚且圆满。
t_en: Our objective upon the Luofu has been completed. Although the situation with Hoolay got a little out of hand... Everything was wrapped up nicely in the end.

t_ch: 非要说有什么遗憾的话……是我没有尽到身为上司的职责，庇护下属的周全。
t_en: If I had to share my regrets... It would be that I was not able to fulfill my duty as a leader and protect the safety of my subordinates.

t_ch: 唉，将军，请不要再给人加深曜青人爱把天聊死的印象了。临行之际，说些快活的话题吧。
t_en: Ah, general, please don't reinforce the Yaoqing stereotype of being conversation killers... Let's talk about something happy before we leave.

t_ch: 你们难道在等我发言吗？我没有感想。走吧，星槎快到了。
t_en: You're not waiting for me to say something, are you? I have no thoughts on the matter. Let's go. The starskiff will be here soon.

t_ch: 等等，我还有两句话要和彦卿小弟说。
t_en: Wait, I would like to say a final word to young Yanqing.

t_ch: 我？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 你在竞锋舰上向呼雷刺出的制胜一剑已臻于妙境，确实不俗。
t_en: The finishing blow you landed on Hoolay on the Skysplitter was masterful, truly remarkable.

t_ch: 不过与敌交锋，还有更多可打磨淬炼的余地。在曜青仙舟，这样的机会遍地都是。若是你有朝一日想飞出罗浮，瞧瞧更广阔的天空，就来曜青仙舟找我吧！
t_en: Although, there is still room for improvement when it comes to your technique in battle. The Xianzhou Yaoqing offers so many opportunities for this, so if you one day have the urge to leave the Luofu and see a broader horizon, come and find me on the Xianzhou Yaoqing!

t_ch: 我…明白了。未来若有机会，请将军一定要不吝赐教。
t_en: I... I understand. If I have an opportunity in the future, I will make sure not to miss out on your personal instruction, general.

t_ch: 好了，诸位，时间不早了，该是我们暂时告别的时候了。
t_en: Alright everyone, time has passed us by. It is time for us to bid farewell for now.

t_ch: 拍张合照再走吧！机巧鸟已经准备好了！
t_en: Let's take a group picture! I've got a cycrane ready!

t_ch: 嘻嘻，来得早不如来得巧，妾身一到就赶上一场好戏。
t_en: Hehe, looks like I made it just in time for a show.

t_ch: 灵砂大人也要离开？
t_en: You're leaving too, Miss Lingsha?

t_ch: 灵砂大人，丹鼎司不能少了你。
t_en: Miss Lingsha, the Alchemy Commission cannot go on without you!

t_ch: 呵呵，妾身也是与各位一样，为朱明仙舟的客人送行的。毕竟我在朱明仙舟学艺多年，受炎庭君照料，妾身也早已算得是半个朱明人了。
t_en: Hehe, I am here to bid farewell to our Zhuming guests as you are. As I've spent so many years studying on the Xianzhou Zhuming and been under the care of Ardens Regia, I am basically a half-native Zhuming person at this point.

t_ch: 全靠灵砂姐姐，远道而来的游客、参与行动的云骑，还有三月都在丹鼎司得到了及时的照顾和治疗，能处理好如此突发事件，除了咱们灵砂姐姐还有谁？
t_en: It's all thanks to Miss Lingsha. The guests from afar, the Cloud Knights who participated in the operation, and March receiving timely care and treatment at the Alchemy Commission — who else but Miss Lingsha could've handled all these harrowing incidents?

t_ch: 哪里哪里，这本是丹鼎司分内之事。
t_en: Not at all. This is all simply the work of the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 将药王秘传、持明龙师盘根错节的势力一一摒除后，再造罗浮丹鼎司的重任就要落在你的肩上了。灵砂，咱们就此别过。
t_en: After eliminating the deeply rooted Disciples of Sanctus Medicus and the Vidyadhara Preceptors, it will fall on you to rebuild the Alchemy Commission. Lingsha, it is time for us to say farewell.

t_ch: 怀炎将军，云璃妹妹一路上小心。也请二位替我向炎庭君问好，灵砂在罗浮仙舟绝不会浪费朱明所学所闻。
t_en: Safe journeys, General Huaiyan and Miss Yunli. I hope that you shall send my regards to Ardens Regia. I will not let all that I learned on the Xianzhou Zhuming go to waste on the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 那么，各位，老夫该走了……
t_en: Well then, it is time for me to be on my way...

t_ch: 等等！我们拍张合照再走吧！还有，我这里准备好的苏打豆汁儿也请一起带走……
t_en: Wait! Let's take a group picture! Oh, and I have mung bean soda here for you all to take with you...

t_ch: 不过那柄宝剑，彦卿把它带了过来，想在这临别时刻……
t_en: But I brought the awesome blade over to use in this moment of farewell...

t_ch: 你可算来了！
t_en: You're here!

t_ch: 听说你有麻烦事要拜托我？
t_en: Heard you've got some problems you'd like a hand with?

t_ch: 与其说是我的麻烦事，不如说说是这位小师傅的…正好铖杰师傅也在这里，要不你自己讲讲？
t_en: It's not so much a problem for me, as it is for this guy... Since Master Chengjie is here now, maybe he can talk about it himself?

t_ch: 我只是一个出不了师的小学徒，我还没资格授徒带队呢。您可别再叫我「师傅」了。
t_en: I'm just a young apprentice who cannot graduate, so I'm not qualified yet to lead any team. Please don't call me "Master."

t_ch: 这样啊，我也只是叫着方便，杰哥你不要介意。
t_en: I'm just saying it for convenience's sake. Don't worry about it, Chengjie.

t_ch: 哎，这位大英雄我记得，公输师傅说你和他携手并肩铲除了困扰司里的妖～魔～鬼～怪～
t_en: Ah, I remember you. You are the great hero Master Gongshu mentioned. He said you helped him fight off all the monnn~stersss~ in the commission.

t_ch: 真羡慕你啊，我什么都做不到，我就只是一个在工造司的混日子的废物…遇到危险也只顾着自己逃命，贪生怕死，一点工造司的匠～人～风～骨～都没有……
t_en: I really envy you. I can't do anything. I'm just a piece of trash wasting my days away at the Artisanship Commission... Every time I come across something dangerous, I flee in fear. I'm such a chicken, and I've got no sense of ar~ti~san~ship~...

t_ch: 你怎么突然这么消极？
t_en: Why are you so self-pitying all of a sudden?

t_ch: 我不是突然这么消极的，自从发现弄丢了老师的手稿之后，我就一直很消极。
t_en: It's not sudden at all. I've been depressed ever since I found out that I lost my master's manuscript.

t_ch: 这也是公输师傅说的？
t_en: Is that what Master Gongshu told you?

t_ch: 公输师傅没有明说，他的心里肯定也是这么想的。
t_en: Master Gongshu didn't say anything to me, but I know he's thinking it deep down.

t_ch: 要是我有匠～人～风～骨～我肯定就不会弄丢老师的手稿。
t_en: If I were a true~ ar~ti~san~, then there's no way I could've lost my master's manuscript.

t_ch: 贪生怕死才是正确的。
t_en: Fear of death is pretty normal.

t_ch: 对呀对呀，对正常人来说，还有比贪生怕死更正确的事吗？
t_en: Yep. Like, seriously, what's more normal than being afraid to die?

t_ch: …可是我只顾着贪生怕死，却把老师的手稿弄丢了，真是千不该万不该啊。
t_en: ...But I was doing just that, and still lost my master's manuscript. It is absolutely unforgivable.

t_ch: 那可是恩师生前留下的遗作，整个银河上下独一无二。
t_en: The manuscript is my master's legacy work of his lifetime, and it's the only copy in the whole cosmos.

t_ch: 唉，老师的手稿本应该在仙舟历史中留下浓墨重彩的一笔…我却只顾着逃出生天，没能将手稿带出来，我真是，千～古～罪～人～
t_en: *sigh* My master's manuscript should have gone down in Xianzhou's history... but I was too busy saving my own skin and didn't take it out with me. I really am utt~er~ly~ use~less~

t_ch: 这么重要的东西不备份？
t_en: Why didn't you make a copy of something so important?

t_ch: 你这么一说，我更加内疚了！
t_en: I feel even worse now!

t_ch: 哎，这要换作是我，太卜司的文件没有保存或者备份，我根本都不敢睡觉。
t_en: Well, if I were in his shoes and didn't save or back up any of the Divination Commission's files, I wouldn't even dare to sleep.

t_ch: 青雀小姐所言极是，可是丹鼎司从来不卖后悔药，就算我现在想备份也晚了。
t_en: What you said is true, but it's too late for regrets. It's impossible to make a copy of it now.

t_ch: 这手稿真的有这么重要吗？
t_en: Is this manuscript really that important?

t_ch: 我才疏学浅，一直以来我都觉得自己还没有足够的资历去面对这份手稿。
t_en: I am not very learned, and I've always felt intimidated and under-qualified to face this manuscript.

t_ch: 这份遗作手稿，是莱恩先生的梦想，我本想自己有一天能够亲手实现…可是，现在我连这样的机会都没有了。
t_en: That manuscript is Master Ryan's magnum opus. I thought that one day I'd be able to actualize its visions myself... But now I won't even get the chance to try.

t_ch: 去把手稿找回来不就解决了？
t_en: Just find that manuscript, and you'll be fine, right?

t_ch: 可是，这工造司里到处都是不受控制的机巧、建木催生的孽物，我…我不敢……
t_en: But the Artisanship Commission is filled with uncontrolled ingenia, and abominations awakened by the Ambrosial Arbor... I dare not...

t_ch: 所以这件事是打算交给我？
t_en: So you'd like me to take over from here?

t_ch: 太好了，{NICKNAME}，你真的愿意帮我找回手稿吗？
t_en: That's great, {NICKNAME}. Are you really willing to help me find the lost manuscript?

t_ch: …这就是青雀叫我来的原因吗？
t_en: ...Is this why you've invited me over, Qingque?

t_ch: 嗯…我可没算到事情会有这样的发展。
t_en: Hmmm... I didn't expect things to turn out like this.

t_ch: 哎呀，这个时候就别管我了，你就帮帮铖杰找回手稿嘛。
t_en: Argh, don't mind me right now. Just help Chengjie find that manuscript.

t_ch: 这样啊，丢了就丢了嘛。
t_en: I see. If it's lost, it's lost.

t_ch: 你说的对，丢了就丢了嘛。
t_en: You're right. What's lost is lost.

t_ch: …青雀小姐？
t_en: ...Miss Qingque?

t_ch: 但是！{NICKNAME}击败了丰饶孽物，夺回了造化洪炉，是工造司的大英雄。帮铖杰找回手稿这么一件小事，你肯定是不会拒绝的吧？
t_en: But! {NICKNAME} has fought off the Abominations of Abundance before, recovered the Creation Furnace, and became a hero at the Artisanship Commission. Helping Chengjie find that manuscript should be a piece of cake that you'd never refuse, right?

t_ch: 不会拒绝的吧？的吧？吧？
t_en: Right? Right? Riiight?

t_ch: …青雀小姐，这些要求这是不是道德绑架呢？
t_en: ...Miss Qingque, are we pushing it too far?

t_ch: 只是让合适的人做合适的事情罢了，你要是没有云骑军保护就自己回去，那也太危险了。
t_en: Just let the right people handle the right things. Besides, it's too dangerous for you to go back alone.

t_ch: 我同意了，但是我有一个条件。
t_en: I agree, but only on one condition.

t_ch: 所以是什么条件呢？
t_en: What condition?

t_ch: 青雀必须跟我一起去。
t_en: You must come with me.

t_ch: 你可别想在这儿偷懒。
t_en: Don't even think about slacking off this time.

t_ch: 我拒绝，谁爱做谁做。
t_en: I'm out. Ask someone else to help.

t_ch: 别吧，有话好商量啊。
t_en: Hey, let's talk this out instead of throwing in the towel, okay?

t_ch: 除非青雀跟我一起去。
t_en: Not unless Qingque comes with me.

t_ch: 怎么可能让青雀一个人偷懒。
t_en: Not fair if you are the only one who gets to slack off.

t_ch: 青雀小姐，那我也拜托你了！
t_en: Miss Qingque, I'm begging you!

t_ch: 哎，我原本的偷懒计划泡空了。好吧好吧，不过关于手稿，你有什么线索吗？那什么设计稿，你懂，都长得一模一样。
t_en: Well, so much for my lazy day plans. Anyways, got any leads on the manuscript? Those manuscript thingies all seem identical to me.

t_ch: 我师傅的梦想是造出谁都没见过的星槎。手稿上如果有「星槎设计」、「龙骨结构」、「星槎引擎」之类的词语，应该就没有错了。
t_en: My master's dream was to create a starskiff that nobody's ever seen before. There should be words like "Starskiff designs," "keel structure," "Starskiff engine" on his manuscript.

t_ch: …你要是说得再复杂些，我们也听不懂，这些就够了。走吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: That's enough for us. If you keep using more of these fancy words, we ain't gonna understand anything. Let's just head out, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 莱恩老师身故以后，他留下的设计图都被工造司入库封存了，应该就放在格物院那儿。
t_en: After Master Ryan passed, his manuscripts were packed away by the Artisanship Commission. It should be placed in the Sapientia Academe.

t_ch: 生前？公输师傅死了？
t_en: During his lifetime? Do you mean Master Gongshu is dead?

t_ch: 遗作？公输师傅啊啊我来晚了…
t_en: Legacy work? What happened to Master Gongshu?

t_ch: 唉，老师的手稿本应该在仙舟历史中留下浓墨重彩的一笔…我却只顾着逃出生天，没能将手稿带出来，我真是，千～古～罪～人～
t_en: *sigh* My master's manuscript should have gone down in Xianzhou's history... but I was too busy saving my own skin and didn't take it out with me. I really am utt~er~ly~ use~less~

t_ch: {F#姐}{M#哥}，先别忙着嚎了。过世的是莱恩先生，是我最早加入工造司时带着我研究的匠人老师，不是现在的公输师傅。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, please get hold of yourself. The one who passed away is Master Ryan, a craftsman who used to be my mentor when I first joined the Artisanship Commission. It's not my current teacher, Master Gongshu.

t_ch: 师傅的手稿可就拜托你们了，一定要平安地带出来呀。
t_en: Please, bring Mr. Ryan's manuscript back in one piece. I leave it in your capable hands.

t_ch: 这里面就是格物院了，如无意外，会有意外。
t_en: This is the Sapientia Academe. Stay sharp and expect the unexpected.

t_ch: 你进去找吧，我帮你看着入口。
t_en: You go in and find the manuscript. I'll keep watch at the door.

t_ch: …哎，你说的对，我进去找找吧。
t_en: Alright. I'll go in there.

t_ch: 我进去找，你来看着入口。
t_en: I'll go in and find the manuscript. You keep watch at the door.

t_ch: 你真是太体贴了。不过，我也没有本事帮你看门。
t_en: You're so sweet. But I'm simply not qualified to help you watch the door.

t_ch: …还是我进去找找，你在这里守着吧。
t_en: ...How about I go in and look for it. You keep watch out here.

t_ch: 我们俩一起进去吧。
t_en: Let's both go in.

t_ch: 太好了，你负责九成的区域，我负责一成，出发！
t_en: Great. You take care of ninety percent of the area, and I'll take the rest. Go!

t_ch: 好一段时间的翻箱倒柜竟没有触动警报，无数乏人问津的文档被找了出来。
t_en: After a long period of rummaging around, the alarm was not triggered, and countless dusty old documents were unearthed.

t_ch: 它们活像是被猫咪把玩后兴味索然地踢进沙发底下的玩具，一辈子也没个见天日的机会。
t_en: They're like toys that have been kicked under the sofa by some uninterested cat, with no chance of seeing the light of day.

t_ch: 除了各种各样的手稿之外，还有一封<color=#dbc291ff>星际和平公司设计师入职邀请函</color>，从日期上看，距离现在已经有<color=#dbc291ff>一百二十多年</color>了。
t_en: In addition to all the manuscripts lying about, there's an envelope titled <color=#dbc291ff>IPC Designer Invite</color>. Judging by the date, it's already <color=#dbc291ff>over <unbreak>120</unbreak> years old</color>.

t_ch: 这黑灯瞎火的，为了图省事，我把所有像是设计稿的文件全部拿出来了。要不我们在这里挑一下，看看到底是哪一份是莱恩的手稿吧？
t_en: It was pitch black in there, so to save trouble, I pulled out everything that looked like a design manuscript. Why don't we sift through these here and see which one of them is Mr. Ryan's?

t_ch: 你也得一起找。
t_en: You have to help me look for it.

t_ch: …我也没打算闲着。
t_en: ...I don't plan on slacking off.

t_ch: 不过你明知道我这人工作态度不太积极，就稍微体贴一下我嘛。怎么感觉你反倒是被太卜大人附身了……
t_en: But you know how I am. I'm not exactly the most enthusiastic worker around here, so do try to cut me some slack. You were just starting to sound like the master diviner.

t_ch: 交给我了。
t_en: Will do.

t_ch: 你心里有我。那我就歇着啦。
t_en: Alrighty then, I'll take a break and leave the rest to you.

t_ch: 有什么不懂的你可以问，不过我也不太熟他们工造司的文档编号就是了。
t_en: If there's anything you don't understand, just ask, but I'm not too familiar with the documentation systems at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 我常听牌友们说「翻乱了工匠的箱子，如同掐住了工匠的脖子」。找到对应的手稿之后，还是得早点儿把其他手稿放回去。不然工造司的人一定会找我麻烦的。
t_en: I often hear from mates that "messing with a craftsman's toolbox is like choking off a craftsman's neck." Once we find the right manuscript, we'll have to put the rest back promptly — otherwise, the Artisanship Commission folks will come after me.

t_ch: 我也感同身受。
t_en: I can sympathize.

t_ch: 我早就看出你是一个富有同理心的人了，我们赶紧开工吧。
t_en: I can already tell that you're an empathetic person, so let's get to work.

t_ch: 不就是找不到东西而已，至于吗？
t_en: Their toolboxes are a bit messy. So what?

t_ch: 没想到你竟然如此不在意，真希望你有勇气当着工匠们的面大声说出来。
t_en: I didn't expect you to care so little. I really hope you can find the courage to speak out about it in front of the craftsman.

t_ch: 我们还是做个好人吧…总之，先赶紧开工吧。
t_en: Let's just put the things back when we are done... Anyway, let's get cracking with our work.

t_ch: 这是一份设计手稿，手稿上解释了「匣里流光」的工作原理。似乎这样的能量武器与一种叫做「岁阳」的妖物有关，设计案署名是公输师傅。
t_en: This is a design manuscript that explains the working principles behind a Luminflux Pyxis, which seems to be related to a kind of demon called Heliobi. And this design is attributed to Master Gongshu.

t_ch: 就是这份设计稿了！
t_en: That's the one!

t_ch: 铖杰说啦！莱恩的愿望是造出星槎，{NICKNAME}，你再想想？
t_en: But Chengjie said that Mr. Ryan's wish is to create a new starskiff, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 下一份。
t_en: Next.

t_ch: 这是一份设计手稿，手稿上详细记录了金人的外观设计。未署名的匠人对审美有所坚持，在设计稿上愤然写下「蠢货们根本什么都不懂，只知道说我的金人丑」。
t_en: This is a blueprint for the aurumatons' exterior design, meticulously documented by an anonymous craftsman who seemed to have an unwavering commitment to aesthetics. This craftsman scrawled in frustration upon this manuscript: "Those fools know nothing of beauty! All they do is besmirch my gorgeous aurumatons".

t_ch: 上一份。
t_en: Previous one.

t_ch: 就是这份设计稿了！
t_en: That's the one!

t_ch: 这上面写的不是星槎，是「金人」。这俩字，很好认的吧？再找找。
t_en: This design talks about aurumatons, not starskiffs. Easy enough to tell apart, no? Keep going.

t_ch: 下一份。
t_en: Next.

t_ch: 这是一份设计手稿，手稿上陈列了星槎的结构设计。设计案提到了一种轻型龙骨的培育模式，设计者提到这样的星槎转弯半径小、速度快、航程远优于市面上的其他星槎。
t_en: This is a manuscript about the design of a type of starskiff, showcasing its structural finesse. The blueprint boasts a cultivation method for a lightweight keel that promises tighter turns, faster speeds, and much longer voyages, far outstripping any other vessel on the market.

t_ch: 上一份。
t_en: Previous one.

t_ch: 就是这份设计稿了！
t_en: That's the one!

t_ch: 看来就是这一份，把它交给我吧。
t_en: This looks like the one. Give it to me.

t_ch: 下一份。
t_en: Next.

t_ch: 这是一份活动设计案，提到了名为「枘凿六合」的空间接合装置。活动设计方考虑到这些魔方状的器械无人问津，决定狠下心来将魔方突兀地放置在洞天的各个角落。
t_en: This is an event proposal that mentioned a spatial fusion device called "Hexanexus." The organizers realized that these Rubik's Cube-like contraptions were not getting any attention, so they decided to take matters into their own hands and awkwardly place them in every nook and cranny of the venue.

t_ch: 「总有人会出于好奇解开它们的，迄今为止的一切努力都不算是白费！」
t_en: "There's bound to be someone who is curious enough to try to solve these. All our efforts will not be in vain!"

t_ch: 上一份。
t_en: Previous one.

t_ch: 就是这份设计稿了！
t_en: That's the one!

t_ch: 哇，{F#大姐}{M#大哥}，注意看，这份手稿是个什么秘密组织的东西。肯定不太对……
t_en: Whoa, look closer! This manuscript looks like something straight out of a secret society. It's definitely not this...

t_ch: 这份手稿，就先由我拿着了，毕竟要是我空手回去，那我不就成了你的挂件了嘛。
t_en: I'll keep this manuscript for the time being. After all, I'll be outshined by you if I go back empty-handed.

t_ch: 让铖杰自己判断吧。
t_en: Let Chengjie decide for himself.

t_ch: 你说得对，还是让他自己看看吧。
t_en: You're right. Let's get him to see it for himself.

t_ch: 感觉没有什么必要。
t_en: That doesn't seem necessary.

t_ch: 它和这份星槎手稿放在一起，总感觉有些关联，我还是带上一起吧。
t_en: It was placed alongside this starskiff manuscript. They seem to have a connection, so let's take it with us.

t_ch: 不过，这份「星际和平公司的设计师入职邀请函」看上去也很重要，应该是和这份星槎手稿放在一起的，要不要也带过去呢？
t_en: However, this "IPC Designer Invite" seems pretty important too. I suppose it's kept in the same place as this starskiff manuscript. We should probably take it with us?

t_ch: 让我来告诉你一个职场的技巧吧，不管是不是上司想要的，只要有，就先带上。太卜大人说要什么，没带来的就是没有，有的我都带来了，就不用来回跑腿了。
t_en: Anyways, lemme tell you a pro tip for the workplace: If it exists, just bring it, even if your boss didn't explicitly ask for it. If the master diviner wants something and I don't have it on me, then I'll just say it doesn't exist, and everything that does exist is already with me. This way, I'll never have to run back for things.

t_ch: 要是铖杰说「啊，我突然想起那份邀请函也很重要」，你要怎么办，我反正不会再回来了，这多累呀。
t_en: What would you do if Chengjie said, "Oh, I suddenly remembered. That invite is also important"? I wouldn't want to keep running back and forth. It's gonna be so tiring.

t_ch: …不过，这种东西他如果不问的话，就直接交给他也不太合适，我就先放在你那里吧。
t_en: ...But if he doesn't ask about this, it's really not appropriate to just hand it over to him, so I'll leave it with you for now.

t_ch: 全是细节。
t_en: Ugh, how troublesome.

t_ch: 你多学着点儿吧。
t_en: Come on, you gotta learn to work smarter.

t_ch: 突然就不想去上班了。
t_en: Suddenly, I don't wanna work on the job anymore.

t_ch: 我其实也每天都这么想…但是工作不能不干。于是我的思维降级，从「如何不上班」改成了「如何偷懒」，你应该多学学我。
t_en: That's how I feel every day actually... But work has to get done. That's why I downgraded my mantra from "How to avoid work" to "How to slack off at work." You should learn from my work philosophy.

t_ch: 嗷呜呜呜——
t_en: Aw woo woo...

t_ch: 这都是什么声音啊……
t_en: What noise is that...?

t_ch: 不好，有怪物靠近了，{NICKNAME}小心！
t_en: Uh-oh, there're monsters nearby. Be careful, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: …糟、糟糕。我有一个坏消息。
t_en: ...D—Darn it. I've got some bad news.

t_ch: 就在刚才，铖杰想要的星槎设计稿变成了「损坏的星槎设计稿」…我们该怎么办？
t_en: The starskiff design manuscript that Chengjie wanted is damaged... What should we do?

t_ch: 实话实说，全部告诉他。
t_en: Just be honest and tell him the truth.

t_ch: 坏了，这下我成了「仙舟历史的千古罪人」了…走吧，我们去谢罪去……
t_en: Oh no, this is bad... I'm gonna get blamed for this and go down in Xianzhou's history as an eternal sinner... Let's just go apologize now...

t_ch: 你就说早就被怪物给破坏了。
t_en: Just say it got wrecked by some monsters long ago.

t_ch: 有道理，全都怪这些怪物，就算我们不来找，它们也会把手稿破坏的。
t_en: That's a good point. We can just blame it all on monsters. Even if we don't find it, they would have been all damaged by monsters.

t_ch: 好，我们就这样告诉铖杰。
t_en: Good, that's what we'll tell Chengjie.

t_ch: 干脆假装我们什么也没找到。
t_en: Let's just pretend that we didn't find anything.

t_ch: 你的意思是说，连这份「损坏的星槎设计稿」也不交给他…这不太好吧？
t_en: Are you telling me that we shouldn't even give him this damaged starskiff design manuscript...? That's wrong, isn't it?

t_ch: 都怪我让你瞻前顾后考虑太多了，我们还是如实转告给他吧，这样最好。
t_en: It's my fault. My concerns have had you overthinking. We should just tell him the truth, and it's for the best.

t_ch: 你们回来了，怎么样找到了我师傅的星槎设计稿了吗？
t_en: You're back. Did you find my master's manuscript for the starskiff?

t_ch: 关于这件事，我想稍微、详细地说明一下。
t_en: About that, I'd like to explain a bit more in detail.

t_ch: 师傅的手稿出事了？不会吧！
t_en: Something happened to the manuscript? No way!

t_ch: 该怎么说好呢……
t_en: How to put this...

t_ch: 青雀尽量将取回手稿的过程描述得惊险无比，铖杰听完之后也大概了解了事情经过。
t_en: Qingque tries to be as dramatic as possible while describing how the manuscript was retrieved. Chengjie seems to have a good idea of the situation after listening to everything.

t_ch: 谢谢你们，至少帮我带回来了手稿里剩下的部分。可是，这都怪我，要是我一开始意志坚定，带上手稿一起逃跑……
t_en: Thank you. At least you managed to bring back the remaining parts of the manuscript. But I should be blamed for this. If I had been more brave and taken the manuscript with me as I fled...

t_ch: 呜呜哇哇哇——
t_en: Boo hoo wahh —

t_ch: 铖杰，你没事儿吧？
t_en: Chengjie, are you okay?

t_ch: 呜呜，我太怂了，我怎么是个这么窝囊的人。口口声声说为了老师的手稿、老师的梦想，我却只是坐在原地等你们帮我取回来……
t_en: Oh, I'm such a wimp. How can I be such a good-for-nothing? I keep saying I'm all about my master's manuscript and his dream, but all I did was sit here, waiting for you guys to do it for me.

t_ch: 连他的手稿都保护不了，我还算什么工造司的工匠。
t_en: I can't even protect a manuscript. What kind of Artisanship Commission craftsman am I?

t_ch: 你别太难过了。
t_en: No need to beat yourself up.

t_ch: 雁过留声，人过留名。莱恩老师，他、他的工匠生涯里就只留下了这份手稿，我却……
t_en: Master Ryan's mighty name reverberates down through the ages. All he left in his career as a craftsman was this manuscript, and yet I...

t_ch: 这和你做不做工匠没有关系吧。
t_en: This has nothing to do with you being a craftsman.

t_ch: 不，我只要拿起工具，就会想到师傅教授了我这些技艺，只要回想这些技艺，就会想到我弄丢了他最后的手稿…我、我做不下去的。
t_en: But every time I pick up my tools, I can only think of my master teaching me these artisan skills. And as soon as I think of these skills, I'll remember that I lost his final manuscript. I—I can't go on like this.

t_ch: 抱歉，出了这样的意外。
t_en: I'm sorry about this accident.

t_ch: 不对，这不是你们的错。师傅的手稿损坏完完全全就是我自己的问题，不怪别人。
t_en: This isn't your fault. Mr. Ryan's damaged manuscript is absolutely positively my fault, so I'll take the blame.

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，我们可不能让他这样消沉下去了呀。你也说点儿什么吧，我要开始感到愧疚了。）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, we can't let him be depressed like this. Say something, or I'll start feeling guilty.)

t_ch: （你也说点儿什么吧，我要开始感到愧疚了。）
t_en: (Say something, or I'll start feeling guilty.)

t_ch: 你来完成手稿中残缺的部分吧。
t_en: You should complete the missing parts of the manuscript.

t_ch: 不行！让我去补全师傅的手稿，就像是刚学会简笔画的小孩去补全著名大画家的遗作一样。
t_en: No! Asking me to fill my master's shoes would be like asking a toddler to duplicate the most famous art piece in the world.

t_ch: 唉，我要是工作时心情低落，就会选个开阔的地方散散心。
t_en: *sigh* If I'm in low spirits at work, I'll simply pick out a wide open space to take a wee stroll.

t_ch: 这样吧，我们先把手稿的事情放一边，去星槎海转换一下心情如何？
t_en: How about this? Let's put the manuscript aside and pick up the mood at Starskiff Haven, huh?

t_ch: 先别想手稿了，换下心情如何？
t_en: Forget about the manuscript for now. Let's take a break!

t_ch: 对的对的，去星槎海如何，那里有好吃的、好玩的，人也很多。
t_en: Yes, yes. Let's go to Starskiff Haven. There's good food, fun stuff, and lots of people there.

t_ch: 转换心情？我不认为转换心情有什么用。不过，这工造司我也待不下去了，无处可去的我，去哪儿都一样。
t_en: I don't think there's any need for that. But I can't stay at the Artisanship Commission anymore. I'm homeless, so it doesn't matter where I go.

t_ch: 那就出发！目标，星槎海。
t_en: Then let's head out! Destination: Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 这工造司我也待不下去了，无处可去的我，去哪儿都一样。
t_en: I can't stay at the Artisanship Commission anymore. I'm homeless, and it doesn't matter where I go.

t_ch: 铖杰你别再垂头丧气了，你看看这里，有美食小吃，可以去不夜侯饮茶，可以逛逛宣夜大街，可解压了。怎么样，你想去哪里？
t_en: Chengjie, don't be so crestfallen. Look, there's food and drink, there's the Sleepless Earl for tea, and there's Starwatcher Avenue for shopping. So, where do you wanna go?

t_ch: 去哪里都可以，青雀小姐你们说了算。
t_en: We can go wherever. Miss Qingque, you call the shots.

t_ch: 那{NICKNAME}你想做些什么吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you want to do?

t_ch: 我们去吃点儿小吃吧！
t_en: Let's go eat some snacks!

t_ch: 走吧，我们去小吃摊上看看有没有什么好吃的。
t_en: Alright, we'll go to a snacks stall and see if there's anything good to eat.

t_ch: 我们去不夜侯喝点儿茶吧！
t_en: Let's go to the Sleepless Earl and order a pot of tea!

t_ch: 我也有点想来一杯仙人快乐茶了，走吧走吧。
t_en: I'm thinking a cup of Immortal's Delight tea would be good. Let's go.

t_ch: 我认识两个说相声的人！
t_en: Oh, oh, I know a comedy duo who does crosstalk!

t_ch: 太好了！我其实经常窝在家里看表演相声的幻戏，可有意思了。
t_en: Cool! Actually, I often binge watch immersias about comedy crosstalks at home, and they're so fun.

t_ch: 哪里都可以，你们说了算。
t_en: We can go anywhere you want.

t_ch: 你怎么也这么消极啊？那就听我的，我们先去小吃摊吃点儿好吃的。
t_en: Why are you being so passive as well? Ah, fine, I'll call the shots then. Let's go and eat those snacks first.

t_ch: 走吧走吧！
t_en: Come on, let's go!

t_ch: 琼实鸟串、貘馍卷、热浮羊奶…还有最近小店推出的盲盒包子！
t_en: Berrypheasant skewers, tuskpir wraps, steamed puffergoat milk... plus the famous mystery steamed stuffed buns that everyone's been eating lately!

t_ch: 铖杰，想吃什么我请你。
t_en: Chengjie, what do you want? It's my treat.

t_ch: 多谢青雀小姐的好意，我…我什么都不想吃。
t_en: Thanks, Miss Qingque. That's very generous. I... I don't want to eat anything.

t_ch: 不想吃不打紧，那你们两个呢，想吃点儿什么？
t_en: It's okay if you don't wanna eat. You two, what do you want?

t_ch: 每样都给我来一份吧。
t_en: Give me one of everything, thanks.

t_ch: 要不你等会儿再吃…我们先带铖杰去别的地方转转。
t_en: How about you wait a bit and eat later... We'll lead Chengjie elsewhere and take a stroll.

t_ch: 我也什么都不想吃。
t_en: I don't want to eat anything, either.

t_ch: 走吧，我们看看铖杰还有没有别的想去的地方。
t_en: Let's go. I wanna see if Chengjie has ideas about any other places.

t_ch: …星槎海…星槎…我只想看看星槎在天上飞。
t_en: ...Starskiff Haven... Starskiffs... I just want to see starskiffs soaring through space.

t_ch: 你还在惦记着手稿吗？
t_en: Are you still thinking about the manuscript?

t_ch: 我现在根本没有办法去想别的事。就带我去看看星槎，然后彻底死心吧。
t_en: I'm simply incapable of thinking about anything else. Take me to see the starskiffs, and maybe I'll get some closure.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去看星槎！
t_en: Let's go. We'll go look at some starskiffs!

t_ch: 既然这样说了，那就走吧，我们去桥上看看星槎。
t_en: Let's go then. We can go to the bridge and watch from there.

t_ch: 客人需要点些什么茶水，梦茗帮客人准备。
t_en: Is there any tea you'd like to drink? I'm your server today.

t_ch: 铖杰就坐这喝茶听书，我再去买点小吃过来……
t_en: Hey, Chengjie, you sit here and enjoy the tea, and I'll slip out to get some snacks...

t_ch: 多谢青雀小姐的好意，我不想喝茶，也不想吃东西。
t_en: Thanks, Miss Qingque. That's very generous. But I don't want any tea or snacks.

t_ch: 我看客人心事重重，梦茗也不便催促。客人先落座，有需要再叫我。
t_en: I can see you have a lot on your mind, and I don't want to pressure you. Please take your seat and call me whenever you need.

t_ch: 总之先来一杯仙人快乐茶吧！
t_en: Anyway, let's get a cup of Immortal's Delight!

t_ch: 虽然我也很想喝，但是就这样让铖杰看着我们俩嘬饮料…不就本末倒置了嘛。
t_en: I want to drink some too, but if we only let Chengjie watch us two drinking... Well, it'd be putting the cart before the horse, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 要不，我们再去别处转转？
t_en: How about we go elsewhere for a walk?

t_ch: 也许现在不是悠然喝茶的时候。
t_en: Perhaps now is not the time to savor a pot of tea?

t_ch: 你说的对，要不，我们再去别处转转？
t_en: You're right. How about we go elsewhere for a walk?

t_ch: …星槎海…星槎…我只想看看星槎在天上飞。
t_en: ...Starskiff Haven... Starskiffs... I just want to see starskiffs soaring through space.

t_ch: 你还在惦记着手稿吗？
t_en: Are you still thinking about the manuscript?

t_ch: 我现在根本没有办法去想别的事。就让我仔细看看星槎，然后彻底死心吧。
t_en: I'm simply incapable of thinking about anything else. Take me to see the starskiffs close up, and maybe I'll have some closure.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去看星槎！
t_en: Let's go. We'll go look at some starskiffs!

t_ch: 既然这样说了，那就走吧，我们去桥上看看星槎。
t_en: Let's go then. We can go to the bridge and watch from there.

t_ch: …说实话，很多观众不知道，咱俩关系这么好，其实是有渊源的，咱俩师出同门。
t_en: ...Honestly, a lot of folks don't know that you and I go way back. We actually studied together.

t_ch: 哎，没错，咱俩是同一个师傅。
t_en: Yep, that's right. We've studied under the same master.

t_ch: 不过，就算是同一个师傅教出来的徒弟，水平也参差不齐。
t_en: But even though we studied under the same master, we aren't exactly on the same level.

t_ch: 哦？你这话里有话。
t_en: Huh? What are you trying to say?

t_ch: 像我这样的就是「学徒」，水平高一些的叫「高徒」，再高一些的叫「爱徒」。
t_en: Take me for example, I'm an "apprentice." Those a bit higher will be called "disciples." Those a bit higher again will be called "prized pupils".

t_ch: 那我呢？
t_en: What about me?

t_ch: …呜呜呜，我就是个不肖之徒。
t_en: ...Boohoo, I'm just a lowly degenerate in comparison.

t_ch: 客人，你怎么还抢我的词儿呢？
t_en: Hey, audience. Please don't steal my line.

t_ch: 铖杰，别影响别人表演啊。
t_en: Chengjie, don't interrupt the performance.

t_ch: …这多少有些触景生情、感同身受了，我们还是别听相声了。
t_en: ...Well, this hits too close to home and might trigger some emotions. Let's just skip this show for now.

t_ch: 要不，我们再去别处转转？
t_en: How about we go elsewhere for a walk?

t_ch: 接得好啊。
t_en: Well said.

t_ch: 你就别起哄了。要不，我们再去别处转转？
t_en: Hey, quit joking around. How about we go elsewhere for a walk?

t_ch: 铖杰，我们还是换个地方吧。
t_en: Chengjie, let's go elsewhere.

t_ch: …星槎海…星槎…我只想看看星槎在天上飞。
t_en: ...Starskiff Haven... Starskiffs... I just want to see starskiffs soaring through space.

t_ch: 你还在惦记着手稿吗？
t_en: Are you still thinking about the manuscript?

t_ch: 我现在根本没有办法去想别的事。就让我仔细看看星槎，然后彻底死心吧。
t_en: I'm simply incapable of thinking about anything else. Take me to see the starskiffs close up, and maybe I'll have some closure.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去看星槎！
t_en: Let's go. We'll go look at some starskiffs!

t_ch: 既然这样说了，那就走吧，我们去桥上看看星槎。
t_en: Let's go then. We can go to the bridge and watch from there.

t_ch: 星槎，应该是所有仙舟住民最熟悉的风景了。
t_en: Starskiffs should be a familiar sight for all Xianzhou citizens.

t_ch: 每天乘坐它在航道里穿梭往返，注视着航道两侧的建筑匆匆后退，百年如一日。
t_en: I crisscrossed the skies on skiffs every day, watching the buildings on either side rush by, just like they have for a hundred years.

t_ch: 很多仙舟人都像我一样，从来不曾意识到「星槎的巧思和技术更新」，只顾着享用它的方便、将它当作是理所当然的存在。
t_en: Lots of Xianzhou people are just like me, who never really thought about "the cleverness and technological advancements behind the starskiff." We just took it for granted and thoughtlessly enjoyed its convenience.

t_ch: 「好的设计是实用的，越是实用的，就越是难以被察觉」，师傅常常这样说。他一生钟情星槎，希望能作出更好的星槎来。
t_en: "Good design is practical, and the more practical it is, the harder it is to be appreciated," is what my master always said. He's loved starskiffs dearly his whole life, and hoped to build an even better model.

t_ch: 我本来也应该成为一个星槎设计师的。可是，我资质太差，还没来得及学成出师，老师就去世了。现在我连他仅剩的手稿也保不住了……
t_en: I should have been a starskiff designer myself. But my grades were too low, and before I could even finish studying, my master passed away. And now, I can't even protect the manuscript that he's left behind...

t_ch: 手稿就像是你们仅剩下的联系。
t_en: That manuscript is all that's left of your bond.

t_ch: 您太高看我了，我可不敢将自己看得这么重要…非要说的话，手稿是师傅和仙舟之间的最后联系。
t_en: You think too highly of me. I don't dare see myself as that important... If anything, the manuscript is what's left of my master's bond with the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 师傅总说「工匠不需要墓志铭」，因为工匠留下的一切都在作品里。
t_en: My master used to say, "A craftsman doesn't need a gravestone," because everything they craft is left behind in the works they make.

t_ch: 可惜，莱恩老师天才而短暂的一生还没能将最后的想法实现，我只觉得很遗憾，我想实现他最后的梦想，我想让师傅在仙舟历史上留下痕迹。
t_en: It's a shame that Master Ryan's brilliant but short life didn't allow him to see his final ideas come to fruition. I feel really sad about it. I want to make his last dream a reality and leave a mark for him in the history of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 看得出你和师傅之间关系很好。
t_en: You and your master seem really close.

t_ch: 不对…是莱恩老师单方面对我太好了，我的水平实在称不上是「他的徒弟」。我缺乏天分、又爱偷闲，他却始终没有放弃我。
t_en: No... Master Ryan was always so nice to me, but my level was technically never good enough to be his apprentice — I lacked the talent and was too lazy, but he still never cast me aside.

t_ch: 等到师傅离世之后，面对他留下的天才设计，我只觉得无力和悔恨。我痛恨自己浪费了学徒时光，不知道该如何实现师傅最后的设计。
t_en: All I felt was powerlessness and regret when confronted by the genius of his work after his passing. I hated that I wasted my apprenticeship, not knowing how to carry out my master's final designs.

t_ch: 唉，咱们爷俩的师徒缘分只有二十来年，实在太过匆匆了。
t_en: *sigh* We've only had twenty years of apprenticeship between us, and it's all gone so fast.

t_ch: 二十年！你跟他学了这么久？
t_en: Twenty years! You've been learning from him for that long?

t_ch: 二十来年，对于短生种几乎四分之一的人生，但对于仙舟人来说还是太短了。公输师傅负责授徒都有几百年了，他的徒弟都开始带徒弟了。
t_en: Twenty odd years —Almost a quarter of a short-life species' existence, but on Xianzhou it's far too short. Master Gongshu took care of disciples for several hundred years, and his disciples have now taken on disciples of their own.

t_ch: 这么一说，一眨眼师傅已经去世了…唔，快一百来年了，我始终没有勇气面对他的手稿。结果……
t_en: Just like that, before I knew it, my master had passed away... It's been almost a hundred years now. I still don't have the courage to face his manuscript. And now...
